LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MILENA MAIONCHI MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER MILENA MAIONCHI'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. THE ANSWER FILED BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST CHRISTOPHER GARRISON AFTER THE WRIT DECISION BY JUDGE QUIDACHAY IS UNTIMELY AND WAS NOT AUTHORIZED BY THE COURT. MR. GARRISON HAS NOT CITED ANY AUTHORITY, NOR IS THE COURT AWARE OF ANY AUTHORITY, ALLOWING A REAL PARTY IN INTEREST IN A WRIT PROCEEDING WHO DIDN'T PARTICIPATE IN THE CASE PRIOR TO THE DECISION TO FILE AN ANSWER AFTER THE DECISION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

LAW AND MOTION 302, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST CHRISTOPHER GARRISON MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL AND FOR CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473 RELIEF. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST CHRISTOPHER GARRISON'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL AND FOR CCP 473 RELIEF IS DENIED. JUDGE QUIDACHAY HAS RETIRED AND IS NOT AVAILABLE TO DECIDE THIS MOTION. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT A REAL PARTY IN INTEREST IN A WRIT PROCEEDING WHO DIDN'T PARTICIPATE IN THE CASE PRIOR TO THE DECISION ON THE WRIT HAS STANDING TO MOVE FOR A NEW TRIAL OR CCP 473 RELIEF AFTER THE WRIT IS DECIDED, MR. GARRISON HAS NOT SHOWN ANY OF THE GROUNDS FOR A NEW TRIAL OR SECTION 473 RELIEF. NEITHER SURPRISE AT THE OUTCOME OF THE WRIT DECISION BY JUDGE QUIDACHAY NOR RELIANCE ON THE CITY TO REPRESENT HIS INTERESTS IS A COGNIZABLE GROUND FOR NEW TRIAL OR SECTION 473 RELIEF. EVEN IF MR. GARRISON HAD SHOWN A GROUND SPECIFIED IN EITHER CCP 657 OR 473, THE MOTION WOULD STILL HAVE TO BE DENIED. IRONICALLY, WHILE MR. GARRISON COMPLAINS OF DELAY BY PETITIONER MILENA MAIONCHI, HE FAILS TO APPRECIATE THAT HE COULD HAVE SOUGHT AN EARLIER HEARING DATE ON THE PETITION OR OPPOSED THE PETITION ON GROUNDS OF LACHES. IN ALL EVENTS, MR. GARRISON HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY AUTHORITY, NOR IS THE COURT AWARE OF ANY, THAT INCREASED DAMAGES OR MONETARY LOSS AS A RESULT OF THE PENDENCY OF A CASE IS "PREJUDICE" AS THAT TERM IS USED IN LACHES CASE LAW. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MILENA MAIONCHI'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is transferred to Department 302 to be heard on February 13, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. CCP 170.6 challenge to the honorable Charles F. Haines on file. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison Motion For New Trial And For Code Of Civil Procedure 473 Relief Of Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Curtis F. Dowling, Esq., Dowling & Marquez, LLP, Telephone 415- 9440444, appearing for Plaintiff;
Robert De Vries, Esq., Telephone 415-5465100, appearing for Real Party In Interest, Christopher Garrison;

Ruing - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison Motion For New Trial And For Code Of Civil Procedure 473 Relief Of Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison

Real Party in Interest Christopher Garrison's motion for new trial and for CCP 473 Relief is denied. Judge Quidachay has retired and is not available to decide this motion. Assuming without deciding that a real party in interest in a writ proceeding who didn't participate in the case prior to the decision on the writ has standing to move for a new trial or CCP 473 relief after the writ is decided, Mr. Garrison has not shown any of the grounds for a new trial or section 473 relief. Neither surprise at the outcome of the writ decision by Judge Quidachay nor reliance on the City to represent his interests is a cognizable ground for new trial or section 473 relief. Even if Mr. Garrison had shown a ground specified in either CCP 657 or 473, the motion would still have to be denied. Ironically, while Mr. Garrison complains of delay by petitioner Milena Maionchi, he fails to appreciate that he could have sought an earlier hearing date on the petition or opposed the petition on grounds of laches. In all events, Mr. Garrison has not provided any authority, nor is the court aware of any, that increased damages or monetary loss as a result of the pendency of a case is "prejudice" as that term is used in laches case law.

Orer is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Petitioner Milena Maionchi Motion To Strike Answer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Curtis F. Dowling, Esq., Dowling & Marquez, LLP, Telephone 415- 9440444, appearing for Plaintiff;
Robert De Vries, Esq., Telephone 415-5465100, appearing for Real Party In Interest, Christopher Garrison;

Ruing - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner Milena Maionchi Motion To Strike Answer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification

Petitioner Milena Maionchi's motion to strike answer to petition for writ of mandate is granted. The answer filed by real party in interest Christopher Garrison after the writ decision by Judge Quidachay is untimely and was not authorized by the court. Mr. Garrison has not cited any authority, nor is the court aware of any authority, allowing a real party in interest in a writ proceeding who didn't participate in the case prior to the decision to file an answer after the decision.

Orer is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
